# Notification test for mcpspy
#
# This script tests mcpspy's ability to handle server-sent notifications
# (messages without an ID) correctly in various formats.

# Setup test files with sample MCP notifications
-- notifications.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notification/system","params":{"type":"info","message":"System starting"}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notification/log","params":{"level":"info","message":"Operation completed"}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req1","method":"normal_request","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notification/progress","params":{"percent":50}}

-- notification_with_requests.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req1","method":"initialize","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notification/status","params":{"status":"connected"}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req2","method":"tools/list","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notification/status","params":{"status":"processing"}}

# Test capturing notifications
cat notifications.json | mcpspy -v -f output_notif1.mcp -- echo "Capturing notifications"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output appears in stderr for notifications
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output_notif1.mcp
stdout "Capturing notifications"

# Verify notifications are properly captured and labeled
grep "notification/system" output_notif1.mcp
grep "notification/log" output_notif1.mcp
grep "notification/progress" output_notif1.mcp
grep "normal_request" output_notif1.mcp

# Test notifications with pretty printing enabled
cat notifications.json | mcpspy -v -pretty -f output_notif2.mcp -- echo "Pretty notifications"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output with pretty printing
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output_notif2.mcp
stdout "Pretty notifications"

# Verify pretty formatting of notifications
grep "\"method\": \"notification/system\"" output_notif2.mcp
grep "\"message\": \"System starting\"" output_notif2.mcp
grep "\"percent\": 50" output_notif2.mcp

# Test interleaved notifications and requests
cat notification_with_requests.json | mcpspy -v -f output_notif3.mcp -- echo "Interleaved messages"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output for interleaved messages
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" output_notif3.mcp
stdout "Interleaved messages"

# Verify order is preserved for interleaved notifications and requests
# We can't easily verify exact order, but we can check that all messages are present
grep "initialize" output_notif3.mcp
grep "notification/status" output_notif3.mcp
grep "tools/list" output_notif3.mcp
grep "\"status\": \"processing\"" output_notif3.mcp

# Test bi-directional communication with color
# (This tests response colors which should be blue)
cat notification_with_requests.json | mcpspy -v -f output_notif4.mcp -- mcpspy -v -f output_notif4_inner.mcp -- echo "Colored responses"
! stderr "error"
# Verify both colors are present in a pipeline
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
stderr "\033\\[34m" # Blue color code for send messages
stdout "Colored responses"
